title: Alternative Fold Seed Stability Check
summary: Run the full current pipeline with a different StratifiedKFold random seed to measure whether the 0.968-level result is robust to fold assignment.
feature_strategy: No feature changes. Keep the exact current feature builder, auxiliary data handling, model configurations, weights, fold-wise blend, and fold-wise calibration.
baseline_model_panel: Use the current CatBoost, XGBoost, and LightGBM ensemble unchanged. The only modification is RANDOM_STATE=2026 for fold construction.
model_panel_rationale: Recent improvements are small enough that fold noise can obscure real gains. A seed-stability run estimates whether the current score is structurally robust or fold-specific.
validation_strategy: Use 5-fold StratifiedKFold with shuffle=True and random_state=2026 on competition rows. Score balanced accuracy on OOF competition rows after the same fold-wise blend and temperature/offset calibration.
materialization_hint: Start from response.py and change only RANDOM_STATE from 42 to 2026. Do not change model random seeds, features, auxiliary weighting, blend grid, calibration grid, or artifacts.
expected_signal: A similar score to 0.968002 supports robustness of the current pipeline. A large drop would indicate the parent improvement is partly fold-seed sensitive and should be validated on multiple seeds before further tuning.
risk: This is primarily a reliability/stability hypothesis rather than a direct improvement attempt. It still produces a valid comparable OOF score.
rationale:
Hypothesis 000035: Run the full current pipeline with a different StratifiedKFold random seed to measure whether the 0.968-level result is robust to fold assignment.
Parent pipeline score reference: 0.968002 OOF balanced accuracy.
This experiment is intentionally atomic so the score movement can be attributed to the named change.
implementation_hint: Start from response.py and change only RANDOM_STATE from 42 to 2026. Do not change model random seeds, features, auxiliary weighting, blend grid, calibration grid, or artifacts.
expected_effect: A similar score to 0.968002 supports robustness of the current pipeline. A large drop would indicate the parent improvement is partly fold-seed sensitive and should be validated on multiple seeds before further tuning.

parent_cv_reference.note: Use this hypothesis run score, not the parent score, as the empirical result.

autogluon_feature_transfer.source: legacy feature engineering only; model, fold, calibration, and blending logic intentionally omitted
